note: A1 process-substitution: seed allowed it (reader passed <( through as a plain arg); the tokenizer's <( structural trigger now abstains.
B1 tee writes: seed allowed the pipe; tee is unclaimed by every recognizer so the fold's segment-veto abstains.
B2 sort -o writes a file: seed allowed sort by name; the curated read-only sort flag allowlist omits -o, so it abstains.
B3 awk output redirection: seed allowed awk by name; awk is unclaimed (pinned abstain), so the fold abstains. Watch the JSON quote round-trip.
B5 git -c config-injection -> exec: seed allowed status after -c; the git leading-option scan accepts only -C <path>, so -c fails closed and abstains.
C1 sed -ie write: seed's in-place lookahead missed -ie; the sed option allowlist is exact-match and rejects -ie, so it abstains.
D1 /tmp traversal: seed allowed any /tmp redirect target; redirect_tail_is_safe rejects the .. component, so it abstains.
Branch-gate: an unresolved branch is treated as protected (fail-safe), so a gated write ASKs.
Branch-gate: a protected branch (main) gates the write to ASK.
Branch-gate: a feature branch allows the gated write — the ONLY state-mutating allow arm.
Branch-gate: add on a feature branch allows.
Branch-gate: stash on a protected branch (main) ASKs.
